evidence.snippet: Founded in 2004, IMSAR is a privately owned business that designs, builds, and supports security and intelligence products. We strive to provide manned and unmanned platforms with the best Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) technology possible.

IMSAR is democratizing radar by making it more usable, affordable, and available than ever before. The low-SWaP, high-performance, multi-mode radar systems bring all-weather strategic capabilities to tactical platforms. These systems complement FMV EO/IR and EW payloads to complete the sensor picture and improve mission effectiveness.

items.notes: SAR/MTI radar pods for UAS; SAR payloads explicitly in category definition

evidence.snippet:
Lisa 3D: Command, Control, and Exploitation
===========================================
Lisa 3D™ is the unified Command and Control (C2) and Processing, Exploitation, and Dissemination (PED) platform for IMSAR radars. Acting as the bridge between the operator and the sensors, it visualizes the imagery generated by the processing server and provides a single, intuitive map-based environment for mission management.

items.notes: Lisa 3D C2/PED software plus ground and airborne processing servers for radar exploitation

HEADQUARTERS & MANUFACturING

Low-SWaP radar systems integrate onto Group 1-5 UAS and manned aircraft without displacing traditional EO/IR payloads. This allows operators to add all-weather radar capability and achieve true Multi-INT collection without compromising platform endurance.

Founded in 2004, IMSAR is a privately owned business that designs, builds, and supports security and intelligence products. We strive to provide manned and unmanned platforms with the best Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) technology possible.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Designs and manufactures SAR/MTI radar pods (payload pods) that integrate onto Group 1-5 UAS platforms. Does not make complete drones.

items.name: NSP-2
items.product_type: Ku-band multimode airborne SAR/MTI radar for Group 1-2 UAS

evidence.snippet:
NSP-2
=====
The NSP-2 is the most accessible entry point for airborne radar, designed for Group 1-2 UAS where every ounce matters. Weighing less than 3.5 lbs, it allows small tactical drones to see through weather and darkness to detect hidden targets that optical sensors miss.

items.name: NSP-3
items.product_type: Ku-band multimode airborne SAR/MTI radar for Group 2-3 UAS

evidence.snippet:
NSP-3
=====
The NSP-3 is the combat-proven industry standard for Group 2-3 UAS. With a low power consumption and a compact footprint, it delivers reliable multi-mode operation for ground and maritime missions without compromising platform endurance or displacing EO/IR payloads.

items.name: NSP-5
items.product_type: Ku-band multimode airborne SAR/MTI radar for Group 3-4 UAS

evidence.snippet:
NSP-5
=====
Designed for UAS platforms with greater payload capacity, the NSP-5 leverages increased transmit power and a larger aperture to extend operational range. It offers enhanced wide-area search and tracking performance, providing a step up in capability for missions requiring deep coverage and precision tracking in complex environments.

evidence.snippet:
Lisa 3D: Command, Control, and Exploitation
===========================================
Lisa 3D™ is the unified Command and Control (C2) and Processing, Exploitation, and Dissemination (PED) platform for IMSAR radars. Acting as the bridge between the operator and the sensors, it visualizes the imagery generated by the processing server and provides a single, intuitive map-based environment for mission management.

items.name: Lisa Ground Expeditionary (LGE)
items.product_type: ruggedized ground processing kit for tactical UAS operations

evidence.snippet:
### Lisa Ground Expeditionary (LGE):
The Complete Tactical UAS Ground Kit

### Lisa Air Max: Standard Rackmount Processing
The Lisa Air Max is a 1U, 18-inch deep rackmount server built for manned aircraft with standard 19-inch rack infrastructure. Operating on 28VDC, it makes onboard ISR missions possible, processing high-resolution imagery and tracks for immediate viewing on a cabin-mounted mission computer.

items.name: Lisa Ground Max
items.product_type: AC-powered 1U rackmount processing server for GCS/fixed-site/maritime

evidence.snippet: ### Lisa Ground Max: Fixed-Site & Maritime Rackmount Processing

items.name: Lisa Air Lite
items.product_type: compact 28VDC airborne processing server for non-standard equipment bays

evidence.snippet:
### Lisa Air Lite: Alternative Form Factor Processing
For manned aircraft where standard rack space is limited or unavailable, the Lisa Air Lite provides identical processing capabilities in a modular, compact form factor. This 28VDC server is designed for flexible installation in non-standard equipment bays while maintaining the high-speed data processing required for real-time missions.

evidence.snippet: Designed, built, and supported in Springville, UT, USA. Scaled operations now yield hundreds of radar systems annually to meet high-volume demand. The focus remains on delivering rapid innovation with high quality, ensuring critical capabilities reach the field on relevant timelines.

The NSP-3 is the combat-proven industry standard for Group 2-3 UAS. With a low power consumption and a compact footprint, it delivers reliable multi-mode operation for ground and maritime missions without compromising platform endurance or displacing EO/IR payloads.

readiness.notes: Site explicitly states hundreds of systems produced annually; NSP-3 described as 'combat-proven industry standard'; in-house manufacturing in Springville UT

tagline: Manufactures Ku-band multimode SAR/MTI radar payloads (NSP-2 through NSP-8) for Group 1–5 UAS and manned aircraft, plus unified ground-segment software and processing servers (Lisa suite). Combat-proven NSP-3 in production; broader certification status (NDAA, Blue UAS) not publicly confirmed. Integrator customer base spans primes and specialized operators.
